request.description:
# Create Album API

Creates a collection of multiple songs.

Access:

Artist only

Endpoint:

POST /api/music/album

### Request Body

{  
"title":"My Album",

```
"musicIds":[
    "music_mongodb_id"
]

 ```

}

### Workflow

1. Verify artist authentication
    
2. Receive selected music IDs
    
3. Create album document
    
4. Store references to songs
    

Database Structure:

Album

{  
title,  
artist:ObjectId,

music:\[  
ObjectId  
\]  
}

### Success Response

201 Created

{  
"message":"Album created successfully"  
}

Concepts:

- One-to-many relationships
    
- MongoDB references

# Get Single Album API

Fetches complete album details.

Endpoint:

GET /api/music/album/:albumId

Example:

/api/music/album/68502abc123

### Workflow

1. Extract albumId from params
    
2. Search album collection
    
3. Populate:
    
    - artist details
        
    - music list
        
4. Return complete album
    

### Success Response

{  
"album":{

```
    "title":"Album",
    "music":[
        {
          "title":"song",
          "uri":"url"
        }
    ]
}

 ```

}

Concepts:

- URL parameters
    
- Database lookup
    
- Nested population

# Get Albums API

Returns available albums.

Endpoint:

GET /api/music/albums

### Workflow

1. Fetch albums
    
2. Populate artist details
    
3. Return simplified album information
    

### Response

{  
"albums":\[

```
    {
        "title":"Album",
        "artist":{
            "username":"artist1"
        }
    }
]

 ```

}

Concepts:

- Query optimization
    
- Select fields
    
- Populate

## Register User API

Creates a new user account in the Audion platform.

Supports two account roles:

- user → normal listener
    
- artist → allowed to upload music and create albums
    

### Endpoint

POST /api/auth/register

### Request Body

Content-Type: application/json

Example:

{  
"username":"artist1",  
"email":"[artist@gmail.com](https://mailto:artist@gmail.com)",  
"password":"123456",  
"role":"artist"  
}

### Workflow

1. Validates incoming user data using Express Validator
    
2. Checks if username or email already exists
    
3. Hashes password securely using bcrypt
    
4. Stores user information in MongoDB
    
5. Generates JWT token containing:
    
    - user id
        
    - role
        
6. Stores JWT inside HTTP cookie
    

### Success Response

Status: 201 Created

{  
"message":"Signed-in",

```
"user":{
    "id":"mongodb_user_id",
    "username":"artist1",
    "email":"artist@gmail.com",
    "role":"artist"
}

 ```

}

### Possible Errors

400 Bad Request

Validation failed

409 Conflict

User already exists

### Technologies Used

- Express.js
    
- MongoDB
    
- bcrypt
    
- JWT
    
- Cookies

Logs out the currently authenticated user.

This endpoint removes the authentication cookie from the client, invalidating the active session on the client side.

## Logout User API

Logs out authenticated user by clearing authentication cookie.

### Endpoint

GET /api/auth/logout

### Workflow

1. Receives logout request
    
2. Removes JWT token cookie from browser/client
    
3. Ends user session
    

### Success Response

200 OK

{  
"message":"User logged out successfully"  
}

### Concepts

- Cookie management
    
- Session handling

## Login User API

Authenticates an existing user and creates a login session.

### Endpoint

POST /api/auth/login

### Request Body

Login using email:

{  
"email":"[artist@gmail.com](https://mailto:artist@gmail.com)",  
"password":"123456"  
}

OR username:

{  
"username":"artist1",  
"password":"123456"  
}

### Workflow

1. Finds user using username/email
    
2. Compares entered password with hashed password using bcrypt
    
3. Generates JWT authentication token
    
4. Stores token inside cookie
    

### Success Response

Status: 200 OK

{  
"message":"User logged in Successfully",

```
"user":{
    "username":"artist1",
    "role":"artist"
}

 ```

}

### Errors

401 Unauthorized

{  
"message":"Invalid Credentials"  
}

### Concepts

- Password hashing
    
- Authentication
    
- JWT token generation

# Get All Music API

Fetches uploaded music from Audion database.

Access:

User / Artist

Endpoint:

GET /api/music/get-songs

### Workflow

1. Auth middleware verifies user
    
2. Fetches music documents from MongoDB
    
3. Populates artist information
    
4. Returns music list
    

### Database Relations

Music Collection

{  
title,  
uri,  
artist:ObjectId  
}

Artist is populated from User collection.

### Response

200 OK

{  
"message":"Music Fetched Successfully",

```
"musics":[
    {
        "title":"Song",
        "artist":{
            "username":"artist1",
            "email":"artist@gmail.com"
        }
    }
]

 ```

}

### Concepts

- MongoDB References
    
- Mongoose Populate
    
- Protected Routes

# Upload Music API

Allows artists to upload music files.

Access:

Artist only

Authentication:

Requires JWT cookie

Endpoint:

POST /api/music/upload

### Request Type

multipart/form-data

Fields:

title

Type: Text

Example:

Night Drive

music

Type: File

Example:

song.mp3

### Workflow

1. JWT middleware verifies user
    
2. Checks user role is artist
    
3. Multer processes uploaded audio file
    
4. Audio file is uploaded to ImageKit cloud storage
    
5. ImageKit returns hosted URL
    
6. Music metadata is stored in MongoDB
    

MongoDB stores:

- title
    
- music URL
    
- artist reference
    

Actual MP3 file is NOT stored in database.

### Success Response

201 Created

{  
"message":"Music Created Successfully",

```
"music":{
    "id":"music_id",
    "uri":"imagekit_url",
    "artist":"artist_id"
}

 ```

}

### Errors

401 Unauthorized

Missing JWT token

403 Forbidden

User is not artist

### Concepts

- Role Based Access Control
    
- Middleware
    
- File Upload
    
- Cloud Storage
